posts.name: Brad England
posts.cooked:
LAFITE VERTICAL - (12/12/2016)

Seven couples joined for a fabulous holiday party/dinner, with ten vintages of Lafite. The classic Lafite signature shined throughout. In order, my top three were 61, 82, and 62.

  • 1961 Château Lafite Rothschild - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    I was wary of the 61, having had a disappointing first bottle of this parcel recently. Surprise. Pretty much a perfect bottle. Lovely fruit, still a lot of intensity, overall a great bottle, and my WOTN in a night of ten Lafite.
  • 1962 Château Lafite Rothschild - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    The 62 was in very good shape, a softer wine, and the fruit was delicious.
  • 1981 Château Lafite Rothschild - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    I actually didn’t taste the 81, but folks were giving it good marks.
  • 1982 Château Lafite Rothschild - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    The 82 Lafite has always been a sentimental favorite for me. Tonight’s bottle was gorgeous, and we all loved it. Having said that, I’m in the controversial camp that says this wine drank better at age 15 than it does at age 34. But I’ll drink it anytime!
  • 1983 Château Lafite Rothschild - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    From magnum. The vintage I was most looking forward to. A lovely wine, that showed as classic Lafite. Yes it’s softer and lighter than it once was, but overall this is a wine you could sit and sip all afternoon.
  • 1985 Château Lafite Rothschild - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    This slightly underperformed its vintage pedigree, but for my tastes still came off as a beautiful bottle, and I was very happy with it. Other folks liked it, but were less enthusiastic than me.
  • 1989 Château Lafite Rothschild - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    This ex-chateau magnum, decanted for four hours, failed to impress. We set it aside for most of the night, but a few hours later it still hadn’t come out of its shell. Interesting, as I’ve had very drinkable bottles of this before.
  • 1990 Château Lafite Rothschild - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    Like the 89 drunk tonight, the 90 failed to live up to its pedigree. I’ve generally liked the 90, but have never liked it as much as others (critics, drinkers). A good wine, and this bottle was in great shape. Just not memorable. Two bottles opened tonight, with consistent results.
  • 1997 Château Lafite Rothschild - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    A vintage I had not tasted previously. Nice nose, good palate, but the finish dropped off quickly, and that’s where this wine lagged. I liked it.
  • 2008 Château Lafite Rothschild - France, Bordeaux, Médoc, Pauillac
    One younger vintage was included in the tasting. The 08 was terrific, if infanticide. This will likely be a star with age.

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: Jim Clary
posts.cooked: Sounds like a fun party. I am selfishly disappointed to here your accolades for the '61. I bought a case at auction 8 years ago, and drank 3 or 4 bottles, all which failed to impress. I finally sold it recently, thinking it was a smart move. Maybe not.

posts.name: Glenn L e v i n e
posts.cooked: Enjoyed reading this as my singular drink of the 82 Lafite was much too young, not even 15!
posts.display_username: Glenn L e v i n e

posts.name: Brad England
posts.cooked:
Jim Clary:

Sounds like a fun party. I am selfishly disappointed to here your accolades for the '61. I bought a case at auction 8 years ago, and drank 3 or 4 bottles, all which failed to impress. I finally sold it recently, thinking it was a smart move. Maybe not.

Jim, if it was Zachy’s around 9/22 of this year, thank you very much. Actually Diane thanks you, as she’s the Lafite fan in the house. Parcel of three. The first bottle was okay, but not great. Interestingly, it had a metalic taste, and that characteristic was the subject of a thread recently, with the general conclusion being it’s brett, which is a totally new concept to me. This second bottle was perfect.

posts.cooked:

posts.name: Jim Clary
posts.cooked: It wasn’t Zachy’s. Glad you had a good experience.

posts.name: Mark Golodetz
posts.cooked: I had a perfect bottle of the 1959 on Friday, and it was utterly spectacular. I have had a lot of good wines this year, but this would be in my top three.

posts.name: Brad England
posts.cooked: Nice. For me, over time the 59’s outperformed the 61’s across the board in Bordeaux. Only have ten bottles of various 61 bdx left, and no 59’s.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: Thanks for sharing, Brad. This was a cool read. I had the 2003 earlier in the year, and 2000 last year, it’s a stunner of a Chateau. But we all know that . . . . Too bad I cannot afford it!

posts.name: Karl K@zaks
posts.cooked: Very nice: thanks!

posts.name: Stuart Roberts
posts.cooked:
Lafite is frustratingly irregular.

Only the '59 and '82 are consistently great and the auction market recognizes that.

posts.name: Stuart Roberts
posts.cooked: I am also thinking that at this point, '59 in Bordeaux is showing better than '61.

posts.name: Jonathan Favre
posts.cooked: YUMMY! Great notes Brad

posts.name: paul hanna
posts.cooked:
Thanks for the notes Brad.

Had the '90 Lafite at lunch the other day, the wine was just sensational.

The '61 is still one of my all time greatest Bdx.

posts.name: William Jones
posts.cooked:
Way to go Brad–
now I have to come up with a new theme for my Feb Tucson Lunch Bunch tasting- might have to have BURGUNDY.
Seriously though, I was very interested in your notes on the 81, 82, 83. Not to hijack the thread, but for me, going on descriptors only, I would have trouble knowing which of the three you would most prefer to drink again, soon( numbers clarify split hairs). I have long had a theory that the “shoulder vintages” i.e. 1 before or after often are very close in quality but suffer from the hype of the “best” vintage. IMHO 81 and especially 83 both support that amateur theory. 58/59. 62/61, 88/89 etc.
Thanks for the notes!

posts.name: Brad England
posts.cooked: Hey Bill. For some reason, I get romantic about certain 83 bdx. It’s not rational. Rational is pronouncing 82 the best, as it frequently is. But I’ve had so many great long days (usually weekend afternoons), with 83’s. Mouton, Pichon Lalande, Lafite, Cheval, Margaux, Palmer.

posts.name: Steve Doi
posts.cooked:
Brad:

Nice set of wines there. I’ve never been moved by the 61 Lafite so glad that you had a stunner. Also happy to see the 62 still showing well. It’s my spouse’s birthyear, so we’ve had a lot of 62’s, and the Lafite has long been a charmer. Still prefer the 62 burgs though.

Hope all’s well with you and come visit us again sometime.

posts.name: Andy B
posts.cooked: A friend opened all of the '62 first growths at his 50th birthday 4 years ago, and the Lafite took the cake for top wine (with the Margaux being a very nice surprise and the Mouton no slouch).

posts.name: David Rudd

I had a 59 Calon Segur last week that was gorgeous. For the first hour, it was funky and I thought it might be bad, but then wow! Amazing wine.
